summary: 2026 ecosystem scan for the Knowledge Plane. (1) Zotero: the dominant MCP is 54yyyu/zotero-mcp (4.1k stars, v0.6.0 June 2026) — hybrid local-API/web-API with built-in ChromaDB semantic search; cookjohn's in-Zotero plugin MCP (Streamable HTTP :23120) and kujenga's web-API-native Python server are the alternates. Zotero 7/8's local API (localhost:23119/api/, GET-only, no auth, loopback-only) works incl. fulltext, but requires the desktop app — irrelevant to a headless CT. Critical finding: WebDAV sync carries ONLY attachments (as .prop/.zip pairs, undocumented format), never metadata, and zotero.org's web API cannot serve WebDAV-stored files — so RFC-0018/ADR-0029's "Zotero MCP reads a WebDAV-synced local copy of the library" is underdesigned: CT 206 must pair web-API metadata reads with a local fulltext shim that unzips its own WebDAV store (a limitation the KP can turn into its differentiator — nobody serves this niche). (2) Obsidian: coddingtonbear's Local REST API now ships a native Streamable-HTTP MCP (v4.1.3, June 2026) but needs the live app; filesystem-direct MCP servers are mature and validate ADR-0029's git-mirror approach and the "Obsidian = recommended viewer, never dependency" OSS posture. Required agent tool surface: list/read/search/append/patch-by-heading-block-frontmatter/tags. BM25 "free via Omnisearch/REST plugin" in RFC-0018 is stale post-git-mirror — retrieval MCP must own its own FTS. (3) Competitors: Khoj (34.5k★, AGPL — license cited as adoption friction; self-host Obsidian-plugin gap; index-only, never writes back), SurfSense (14k★, Apache-2.0, connector moat + 2-tier hybrid RAG w/ rerankers), Reor archived Mar 2026 (local-first desktop apps die alone), RAGFlow (heavyweight: ES+MySQL+MinIO+Redis, 16-32GB RAM — anti-pattern for homelab), Open WebUI (agentic-RAG direction, chunking woes), Karakeep (bookmark-everything + AI tagging + RSS + rule engine + MCP; the adjacent-not-competing reference). Nobody ships a git-native librarian over Zotero+vault: closest are zotero-arxiv-daily (5.6k★ digest via GitHub Action; embedding-similarity interest model, recency-weighted, self-described "very simple"), Research Hub MCP, llm-for-zotero, SeerAI — all bolt-ons, none canonical/derived, none behavioral-signal-driven. The gap is real. (4) Agent harness: 2026 pattern is headless `claude -p` + cron/systemd with --allowedTools/--max-turns/budget caps and CLAUDE.md standing orders; GitHub Agentic Workflows' "safe outputs" doctrine (agent proposes via branch, a separately-privileged deterministic process disposes) is the proven security model for the librarian's proposals/ workflow — KP should encode it as CI schema-gates on proposal branches. Anthropic's cloud Routines exist but violate local-first; design librarian as harness-agnostic prompts/skills/contract (Claude Code recommended, not required — mirrors the Obsidian posture). (5) Packaging: winning conventions are compose profiles via COMPOSE_PROFILES (cpu/gpu variants), Immich's image-tag-suffix + extends-file pattern for optional accel, Paperless's _FILE docker-secrets + interactive setup script, .env-with-sane-defaults. Table stakes: one-command compose up, OpenAI-compatible LLM endpoint env (pluggable — llm-gateway/Ollama both just work), hybrid retrieval with citations, CPU-only default, 15-minute quickstart. Differentiators for OSS KP: canonical/derived git-native spine (indices disposable — no competitor dares), proposal-branch librarian with safe-outputs separation, the Curio seam (frontmatter-v1 ingestion + events-v1 behavioral interest model — richer signal than any competitor's), Zotero WebDAV-fulltext depth, supports/refutes claim graph (find_contradicting has no equal anywhere), MCP Registry publication (18k+ servers = discovery channel), stateless Streamable-HTTP servers per the MCP 2026-07-28 spec RC.
facts: Zotero MCP leader: 54yyyu/zotero-mcp — 4.1k stars, v0.6.0 (2026-06-22), pip/uv/pipx/Docker; ~40 tools across search (incl. zotero_semantic_search, zotero_search_by_citation_key via BetterBibTeX), content (fulltext, annotations, PDF outline), writes (add by DOI/URL/ISBN/BibTeX, collections, dedupe/merge), relations, Scite citation-intelligence; hybrid architecture: local API primary for reads, web API for writes (local JS API is read-only), direct sqlite reads for its semantic index; semantic backends: all-MiniLM-L6-v2 local default, OpenAI, Gemini, Ollama (nomic-embed-text, bge-m3) — ChromaDB store, stale-embedding pitfalls on model switch (https://github.com/54yyyu/zotero-mcp)

Alternate Zotero MCPs: cookjohn/zotero-mcp — a Zotero PLUGIN with embedded MCP server, Streamable HTTP on port 23120, 20 tools, requires desktop app running, 968 stars, v1.5.0 June 2026 (https://github.com/cookjohn/zotero-mcp); kujenga/zotero-mcp — web-API-native Python (pyzotero), works fully headless (https://github.com/kujenga/zotero-mcp)

Zotero 7/8 local API: http://localhost:23119/api/ mirrors web API v3; GET-only ('Write requests are currently unsupported', writes promised in an upcoming version); NO authentication — anyone on loopback reads the library, so any network exposure needs its own auth layer; unique extras: saved-search execution, file:// attachment redirects; fulltext endpoint (/items/KEY/fulltext) confirmed working Oct 2025+ (https://www.zotero.org/support/dev/web_api/v3/local_api, https://groups.google.com/g/zotero-dev/c/YhGdRDWtf8g)

LOAD-BEARING: Zotero WebDAV sync stores ONLY attachments, as paired KEY.prop + KEY.zip files (zipped, encoded filenames); metadata never touches WebDAV — it rides zotero.org sync. And files stored on WebDAV are NOT retrievable via the zotero.org web API or web library ('only files stored in Zotero File Storage are available via zotero.org'); WebDAV file sync is officially undocumented — 'you can figure it out by reading the Zotero source... third-party apps have done so — but we don't provide support' (https://www.zotero.org/support/kb/webdav_services, https://forums.zotero.org/discussion/28207, https://forums.zotero.org/discussion/89098)

Zotero WebDAV corruption pitfalls: orphan .zip without .prop blocks sync; 'Invalid attachment hash undefined' = corrupt server file, fix is delete the pair + Reset File Sync History; sync settings (method + URL) must match on ALL devices or files silently don't transfer; never place the Zotero data directory itself in synced/cloud storage (database corruption); server must support full GET/PUT/DELETE/PROPFIND/MKCOL/OPTIONS (https://www.zotero.org/support/kb/files_not_syncing, https://forums.zotero.org/discussion/126371, https://www.zotero.org/support/sync)

Obsidian MCP: coddingtonbear/obsidian-local-rest-api v4.1.3 (June 2026, 2.6k stars) now ships a NATIVE MCP server (Streamable HTTP at https://127.0.0.1:27124/mcp/, bearer API key, self-signed TLS) — full CRUD, PATCH targeted at heading/block-ref/frontmatter, JsonLogic search, periodic notes, command execution; but requires the live desktop app (https://github.com/coddingtonbear/obsidian-local-rest-api)

Filesystem-direct vault MCP servers are a mature 2026 category (obsidian-mcp, MCPVault, phate45/mcp-vault): vault-path-only setup, no plugin/API key, work without Obsidian running — validating ADR-0029's git-mirror choice and the OSS requirement that Obsidian stay a recommended viewer, not a dependency; canonical research-agent tool surface across the ecosystem: list_files, get_file_contents, search (full-text + structured), append_content, patch_content (heading-anchored), frontmatter/tag ops (https://github.com/MarkusPfundstein/mcp-obsidian, https://github.com/phate45/mcp-vault, https://mcp.directory/blog/obsidian-mcp-complete-guide-2026)

Khoj: 34.5k stars, AGPL-3.0 (repeatedly cited as adoption blocker vs SurfSense Apache-2.0 / Open Notebook MIT), self-host = Postgres+pgvector + server; strongest at scheduled automations + personal second-brain; known gaps: Obsidian plugin functionality restricted for self-hosters (cloud-first drift), index-only — never writes back to the vault, last commit ~2 months stale vs SurfSense's daily activity (https://github.com/khoj-ai/khoj, https://openalternative.co/compare/khoj/vs/surfsense, https://docs.khoj.dev/get-started/setup/)

SurfSense: 14k stars, Apache-2.0, most active; moat = connector breadth + two-tier RAG (hybrid semantic+keyword retrieve, then rerank) — the retrieval bar an OSS KP must meet (https://openalternative.co/compare/khoj/vs/surfsense)

Reor (local-first AI notes desktop app, Ollama+Transformers.js+LanceDB) was ARCHIVED 2026-03-07 — cautionary: solo local-first desktop apps die; systems built as composable services + published contracts outlive apps (https://github.com/reorproject/reor)

RAGFlow: requires Elasticsearch/Infinity + MySQL + MinIO + Redis, 16GB RAM min/32 recommended, ~7GB unpacked image — the heavyweight anti-pattern the KP's compose packaging must visibly avoid (https://ragflow.io/docs/configurations, https://github.com/infiniflow/ragflow)

Open WebUI knowledge: moving to agentic RAG (model decides when/how to search, retries queries); documented weaknesses: markdown-header chunking yields context-starved fragments; uniform RAG ignores user intent; agentic RAG needs tool-capable models (https://docs.openwebui.com/troubleshooting/rag/, https://github.com/open-webui/open-webui/discussions/17331)

Karakeep (ex-Hoarder) v0.31.0 (Feb 2026): bookmark-everything with AI auto-tagging (Ollama-capable), RSS auto-ingest, rule engine (auto-tag/archive/sort), REST API + webhooks, CLI, browser extensions, community MCP server — the adjacent product proving demand for AI-organized personal ingest, but DB-owned, not git-native, no interest model (https://github.com/karakeep-app/karakeep/releases, https://docs.karakeep.app/)

No one ships a git-native 'librarian over your own Zotero+vault.' Closest: zotero-arxiv-daily (5.6k stars — daily arXiv digest via free GitHub Action; interest model = abstract-embedding similarity weighted-averaged over your Zotero library with recency weighting + glob ignores; authors admit 'the recommendation algorithm is very simple'), llm-for-zotero (agent inside Zotero: MinerU PDF parse, auto-tag/organize/import), SeerAI (in-Zotero agent plugin w/ MCP, OCR, semantic scholar), Research Hub MCP (Zotero+Obsidian+NotebookLM clusters/briefs). All are bolt-ons to one tool; none do canonical/derived, proposal branches, or behavioral signals (https://github.com/TideDra/zotero-arxiv-daily, https://github.com/yilewang/llm-for-zotero, https://github.com/dralkh/seerai)

Agent-harness 2026: headless `claude -p` under cron/systemd is the standard local pattern — --allowedTools patterns, --max-turns, --max-budget-usd are the safety triad; CLAUDE.md carries standing orders per run; Anthropic Managed Agents/Routines offer cloud cron but violate KP local-first (https://www.mindstudio.ai/blog/claude-code-headless-mode-autonomous-agents, https://claudefa.st/blog/guide/development/scheduled-tasks)

Proposal-branch security doctrine (GitHub Agentic Workflows, public preview June 2026): 'The agent proposes. A different process disposes. That separation is the whole security model' — privileged effects go through 'safe outputs' validated by a separately-permissioned job; also `agent/*` branch-namespace convention. Direct precedent for KP proposals/ branches + deterministic CI schema-gate before merge (https://github.blog/changelog/2026-06-11-github-agentic-workflows-is-now-in-public-preview/, https://github.blog/ai-and-ml/generative-ai/agent-pull-requests-are-everywhere-heres-how-to-review-them/)

Packaging conventions that win: COMPOSE_PROFILES env selecting cpu/gpu-nvidia/gpu-amd profiles (hwdsl2/self-hosted-ai-stack); Immich: ML in a separate container with image-tag suffix variants (:release-openvino etc.) + hwaccel.ml.yml extends-file, CPU default; Paperless-ngx: _FILE-suffix docker-secrets convention for every env var + an interactive setup script that writes files, pulls, starts, creates the superuser; Karakeep: dir + compose + .env quickstart (https://docs.immich.app/features/ml-hardware-acceleration/, https://docs.paperless-ngx.com/setup/, https://github.com/hwdsl2/self-hosted-ai-stack, https://docs.karakeep.app/installation/docker/)

MCP ecosystem status: Registry launched Sept 2025, 18k+ servers by Mar 2026 (publication = free discovery channel for KP's servers); spec RC 2026-07-28 (final July 28) removes protocol-level sessions → stateless Streamable-HTTP servers behind ordinary load balancers, adds Mcp-Method/Mcp-Name routing headers and ttlMs/cacheScope caching on list/read results — build KP MCP servers stateless against this (https://blog.modelcontextprotocol.io/posts/2026-07-28-release-candidate/, https://zylos.ai/research/2026-03-08-mcp-remote-evolution-streamable-http-enterprise-adoption)

Interest-model research precedent: implicit-signal ranking (clicks, dwell, scroll) over explicit ratings is long-validated (NectaRSS); 2026 recommender literature is moving to LLM/agentic paradigms — Curio's events-v1 (subscribed/read/starred/read_later/promoted JSONL with cursors) is exactly the implicit-signal substrate competitors lack (https://arxiv.org/pdf/cs/0610019, https://arxiv.org/html/2504.16420v1)

managed-postgres Postgres ships pgvector as a bundled extension (industry-standard in its stack) — RFC-0018's 'may already ship pgvector' is all but confirmed; verification is a one-liner (SELECT * FROM pg_available_extensions WHERE name='vector') at Phase 3

Better-BibTeX exposes local HTTP export endpoints (/better-bibtex/collection|library|item/.../export on :23119) and deterministic citation keys in an attached sqlite table — the citation-key bridge between vault literature notes and Zotero items that 54yyyu/zotero-mcp already surfaces as zotero_search_by_citation_key (https://forums.zotero.org/discussion/119427/betterbibtex-zotero-7-citation-key-api, https://zread.ai/retorquere/zotero-better-bibtex/patches)
open_questions: CT 206 metadata path: web-API-only (pyzotero against zotero.org — mind rate limits, Last-Modified-Version polling etiquette, and a cache) vs periodically shipping a zotero.sqlite snapshot from the Mac for local reads (version-fragile, schema undocumented) vs both. The WebDAV store alone cannot answer metadata queries — something must.

Fulltext-from-WebDAV shim: the .prop/.zip format is stable-but-unsupported upstream; does the KP unzip on-demand, or run an extraction cron into a derived attachment cache keyed by item hash? Who owns re-extraction when a .prop hash changes?

Adopt 54yyyu/zotero-mcp wholesale (and disable its ChromaDB semantic search to avoid a second, divergent embedding store violating single-derived-index discipline) vs wrap kujenga's web-API-native server vs write the thin Zotero MCP inside the KP monorepo for contract control?
Zotero local API write support 'in an upcoming version' — if it lands, does the headless calculus change at all? (Probably not — still desktop-bound — but watch it.)
OSS license for the knowledge-plane repo: Khoj's AGPL is documented adoption friction; Apache-2.0/MIT maximizes 'plug Curio into it' replication — needs Alex's call.

Which retrieval FTS engine replaces the now-stale 'free BM25 via Omnisearch': SQLite FTS5 (zero-dep, matches sqlite-vec default) vs tantivy (faster, Rust dep) — interacts with the LanceDB-vs-pgvector Phase 3 decision and the OSS pluggability story (sqlite-only core profile vs postgres profile).
MCP spec 2026-07-28 finalizes July 28 — build KP servers against the RC now (stateless, Mcp-Method headers) or ship on 2025-06-18 stable and migrate? Ten-week validation window is open.
Does Curio's events-v1 webhook sink exist early enough for the interest model, or does Phase 5 start from the file-sink JSONL only (roadmap.json Phase 3 timing question — check designs.json kp_seam)?
CPU-only reranker viability on the M5 Ultra (e.g., bge-reranker-v2-m3 at digest-batch sizes): worth an optional compose profile, or does BM25+vector+graph fusion suffice? Benchmark at Phase 3.
Where does the librarian's interest model live so it stays derived-but-inspectable — a versioned YAML/markdown artifact in the vault (git-diffable, Alex-editable) vs an opaque row in the index DB?
stale_or_wrong: UNDERDESIGNED (RFC-0018 placement table + ADR-0029 decision): 'Zotero MCP reads a WebDAV-synced local copy of the library.' WebDAV sync never carries the library — only attachments, as undocumented .prop/.zip pairs; zotero.sqlite and all metadata live in zotero.org sync, and the web API cannot serve WebDAV-stored files. CT 206 therefore needs a two-channel design: metadata via zotero.org web API (headless-safe), fulltext via a local shim over its own WebDAV zips. Not a placement change — the colocated-on-CT-206 choice is actually strengthened (only the WebDAV host can serve those files) — but the data path as written doesn't exist.

STALE (RFC-0018 Design, Access plane): 'a Zotero server (reads zotero.sqlite directly, no live app needed)' — the sqlite-reading approach requires the sqlite file, which never reaches G11 via WebDAV; the 2026 ecosystem's headless path is web-API-native (kujenga) or hybrid (54yyyu). Direct sqlite reads are also schema-version-fragile and lock-contended if Zotero runs.

STALE (RFC-0018 Design, Index plane): 'full-text/BM25 (free via the Obsidian REST plugin/Omnisearch)' — contradicted by ADR-0029's own amendment dropping the live desktop app; both Omnisearch and the REST plugin require Obsidian running. The retrieval MCP must ship its own FTS (SQLite FTS5/tantivy) over the git mirror.

STALE (RFC-0018 Design, Access plane): 'an Obsidian server (needs the live desktop app — see Open Questions)' — superseded within the same document set by ADR-0029's git-mirror resolution; the RFC body was never re-edited. Also newly relevant: coddingtonbear's plugin now embeds a native Streamable-HTTP MCP (v4.x), so the Mac-side (Alex's live vault) could get a first-class MCP for free even while the server side stays filesystem-based — a two-surface option the docs don't contemplate.

WEAKENED (RFC-0018 Alternatives): 'mature options exist (zotero-mcp-server/zotero-cli, ZotPilot...)' — the actual 2026 center of gravity is 54yyyu/zotero-mcp (4.1k stars, monthly releases) plus cookjohn's plugin-MCP; ZotPilot is not a factor. More importantly its built-in ChromaDB semantic search overlaps the KP's Phase 3 index plane — using it unmodified would create a second derived embedding store outside the KP's index discipline; the RFC's 'only retrieval-fusion is custom' claim needs a decision about disabling/replacing that subsystem.

SOFT-CONFIRMED (RFC-0018 Reuse): 'managed-postgres's Postgres may already ship pgvector' — managed-postgres bundles pgvector by default; the open question can be downgraded to a one-line verification, and the pgvector lean is further supported by the cross-host (G11 queries pve) topology ADR-0029 already noted. For the OSS distribution, though, Postgres must be a compose profile, not a requirement — a sqlite-vec/FTS5 single-container core keeps the RAGFlow-style heavyweight trap out of the quickstart.

UNDERDESIGNED (RFC-0018 Librarian plane): proposal branches are specified but with no disposal-side machinery; the 2026-proven pattern (GitHub Agentic Workflows 'safe outputs') requires a separately-privileged deterministic validator between agent proposal and merge. KP already owns the idiom (this vault's CI gate suite) — the librarian design should name it: frontmatter-schema + checksum + link-integrity CI gates on proposals/*, merge rights withheld from the librarian's credentials.

UNDERDESIGNED (RFC-0018 interest model): 'topic/claim vectors with evidence weights' has no signal source specified; the strongest available signal is Curio's events-v1 behavioral stream (read/starred/promoted), which the RFC predates in specificity — the native-Curio directive means the interest model should be explicitly contracted to consume events-v1, with zotero-arxiv-daily's recency-weighted library-similarity as the cold-start baseline.
